title: study.yaml (v3/v4)
description: Schema for a pbg-superpowers study definition (studies/<slug>/study.yaml). Accepts schema_version 3 (legacy: baseline is a non-empty list, observables/variants are top-level) and 4 (current: conditions{baseline,variants,model_settings,expert_inputs} groups them, plus the second-tier narrative fields — report, study_card, biological_summary, literature_anchors, design_pivot_required, conclusion_verdicts, runtime, enforced_params, follow_up_of — that the dnaa investigation evolved). All v4 fields are optional; a v3 spec validates unchanged. New scaffolds should write schema_version: 4 with the narrative-spine fields present (empty or commented) so they prompt the author to fill them in.

schema_version.description: 3 = legacy v3 (baseline/variants/observables at top level). 4 = current (conditions{} grouping + narrative-spine fields populated). New scaffolds emit 4.

name.description: Kebab-case slug matching the enclosing directory name (e.g. dnaa-01-expression-dynamics).

created.description: ISO-8601 date when this study file was created (YYYY-MM-DD).

status.description: Lifecycle status. Two enums coexist: the v3 author-set values (draft, in-progress, completed, archived) and the dashboard's runtime sub-state values within a phase (planned, running, ran, complete, failed, invalid). The latter are what the parallel-agent's canonical studies use alongside the top-level `phase:` field. NOTE: superseded for new specs by the six independent multi-axis status fields below (design_status, implementation_status, simulation_status, evaluation_status, gate_status, expert_review_status). Kept for v3 back-compat — existing studies that set only `status:` continue to validate. Pass A infrastructure-feedback roadmap.

design_status.description: Multi-axis status (Pass A). How mature the spec/design is. Independent of all other axes. Optional.

implementation_status.description: Multi-axis status (Pass A). Whether the code that backs the study (composites, processes, listeners) has been written. Independent of all other axes. Optional.

simulation_status.description: Multi-axis status (Pass A). Whether the simulations described in simulation_set have been executed. Independent of all other axes. Optional.

evaluation_status.description: Multi-axis status (Pass A). Whether behavior_tests have been run against the simulation output. Independent of all other axes. Optional.

gate_status.description: Multi-axis status (Pass A). The pipeline-gate verdict — drives downstream unblocking. When set, the dashboard renders this as the headline status pill. Independent of all other axes. Optional.

expert_review_status.description: Multi-axis status (Pass A). Whether a domain expert has signed off on the study's framing/conclusions. Independent of all other axes. Optional.

phase.description: Optional declaration of the study's current high-level phase. The 5-phase lifecycle (Design → Build → Simulate → Evaluate → Decide) is the canonical coarse-grained coordinate; the existing `status:` field captures the finer-grained runtime state within a phase. The enum is capitalized — the dashboard's study-detail.html template reads `study.phase` directly. If absent, the dashboard infers the phase from status + the presence of artifacts (spec fields filled, runs.db exists, etc.). See docs/concepts/vivarium-dashboard-model.md for the canonical 8-section structure.

objective.description: One-paragraph statement of what this study measures or demonstrates.

description.description: Extended background / context. Multi-line YAML strings are fine.

hypothesis.description: Testable prediction; optional for exploratory studies.

conclusion.description: Filled in after analysis. Leave null while status is draft/in-progress.

baseline.description: CANONICAL: always a non-empty list, even if there is only one architecture. Each entry is a named simulation configuration. Canonical since v3; do NOT use the old single-dict form.

name.description: Short identifier for this baseline run (e.g. 'baseline', 'wild-type').

composite.description: Dotted Python import path to the composite factory function (e.g. v2ecoli.composites.baseline.baseline).

params.description: Free-form keyword arguments forwarded to the composite factory.

variants.description: List of named parameter perturbations applied on top of baseline.

interventions.description: Structural modifications (add/remove process, rewire) applied before running.

observables.description: Store-path bindings to observe during runs.

visualizations.description: Declared visualizations for this study's outputs.

runs.description: DEPRECATED in v4. Use runs.db (per-study SQLite) as the source of truth — runs_meta + history. F2 made the DB canonical; this field remains only for v3 back-compat and is no longer written by /pbg-study run-baseline. Will be removed in a future schema bump.

tests.description: Either a test-configuration block (auto_discover, data_source, pytest_args — usually auto-managed) or a v4 per-test item array (the band/pass_if shape used by dnaa-style studies). Both forms keep additionalProperties:true for forward compat.

data_source.description: Where the dashboard reads test inputs from. Common values: runs_db, emitter, file, latest_run. Free-form for forward compat across dashboard versions.

oneOf.description: v4 per-test items (the band/pass_if shape used by dnaa-style studies). Each item declares a named test with a measure, acceptance criterion, and optional citation.

classification.description: Common values: primary, secondary, supporting, diagnostic. Free-form for forward compat.

cites.description: bib_keys (in references/papers.bib) sourcing the acceptance band or criterion for this test.

references.description: References supporting this study. May be an object (keyed sections) or an array of {file, section?} items.

oneOf.description: Sectioned references object: keys are section names (e.g. 'expert', 'bib_keys', 'claims'), values are lists of strings.

implementation_tasks.description: Work items for implementing this study. May be a string (free-form) or a list of strings.

parent_studies.description: Studies that must reach a given condition before this one runs. Each item is either a bare slug string (legacy form; normalized to {study: <slug>, condition: tests-passed}) or an object {study, condition: tests-passed | ran | complete}. Read by the dashboard's /api/investigations to compute `blocked` + `blocked_by` per study and to topologically sort the Studies tab.

oneOf.description: Legacy bare-string form: parent slug. Normalized to {study: <slug>, condition: tests-passed} at load time.

condition.description: When the parent satisfies this condition, this study is unblocked. tests-passed = parent.tests.last_results.passed > 0 AND failed == 0. ran = parent.status in {ran, complete}. complete = parent.status == complete.

expected_behavior.description: List of testable behavioral expectations. Accepts two shapes: (1) legacy free-form strings for exploratory studies, or (2) structured DSL dicts with (name, en, given, measure, expect) for automated assertion via vivarium_dashboard.lib.expected_behavior.evaluate().

oneOf.description: Structured DSL entries. Each entry maps one English sentence to a (given, measure, expect) triple.

name.description: Stable kebab-case slug. Used as pytest node ID and dashboard label.

en.description: One-sentence English description. Shown on the Overview tab.

given.description: Preconditions / setup. Common keys: run (baseline | variant), variant (name), window (full | second_half | post_initiation_10min).

measure.description: What to compute from the history. kind is one of: bulk_count | listener_path | listener_sum | xy_correlation | event_count | concentration. Evaluator catches unknown kinds at run time (forward-compatible).

kind.description: Measure primitive. See vivarium_dashboard.lib.expected_behavior for full list.

reduce.description: How to aggregate the series: series | median | mean | first_and_last | pre_post_event_ratio | top_quartile_vs_bottom_quartile.

expect.description: Assertion on the reduced measure. op is one of: in_range | rolling_cv_below | ratio_at_most | ratio_at_least | monotonic_decreasing | pearson_below | pearson_above | pre_post_event_ratio.

op.description: Comparison operator. Evaluator catches unknown ops at run time.

status.description: implemented: test runs normally; stub: xfail pending upstream gap; gated: xfail with requires reason.

requires.description: Cross-references to gaps/listeners/variant hooks this entry depends on.

notes.description: Free-form notes shown as xfail reason when status is stub.

expert_questions.description: Advisory: open questions to pose to domain experts. Free-form strings or list.

purpose.description: Section 1 of the canonical 8-section structure. Replaces v3 question + hypothesis + objective. Sub-keys: question, mechanism, expected_outcome (all free-form strings).

pipeline_gate.description: Section 2 of the canonical 8-section structure. Captures study-level DAG semantics: which parent studies must be done, which child studies become unblocked, and what counts as 'safe to proceed'. Supersedes parent_studies as the source-of-truth (parent_studies remains as a v3 back-compat shim).

prerequisites.description: List of parent dependencies. Each entry is one of: (a) a bare slug string (legacy form), (b) an object {study, condition: tests-passed | ran | complete} (the existing object form, identical to parent_studies entries), or (c) Pass A extended object form with required_gate_status / outputs_used / artifact_hashes for stale-propagation and downstream-validation. All new fields are optional.

condition.description: When the parent satisfies this condition, this study is unblocked. Same semantics as parent_studies[].condition.

required_gate_status.description: Pass A extension. The minimum gate_status the parent must reach for this dependency to count. Independent of `condition` — both can be specified; the dashboard ANDs them.

outputs_used.description: Pass A extension. Named outputs (readouts, derived metrics) from the parent that this study actually consumes. Lets the linter detect over-broad dependencies and lets the dashboard show targeted dataflow edges.

artifact_hashes.description: Pass A extension. Hashes of upstream artifacts this study was last validated against. When the parent regenerates an artifact whose hash diverges, the dashboard flips this study's gate_status to `stale`. All sub-fields optional.

enables.description: List of child study slugs unblocked when this study's primary tests pass.

proceed_condition.description: Free-form description of when downstream studies may safely start.

blocks_until_resolved.description: Free-form description of what happens to the pipeline if primary tests fail.

gate_evaluator.description: Declarative evaluator that backs the top-level gate_status pill. `expr` references behavior_test names; the dashboard evaluates it after the latest run and writes `result` + `blocked_by`. Lets the dashboard compute gate state without parsing the prose in proceed_condition / blocks_until_resolved.

expr.description: Boolean expression over behavior_test outcomes. Common form: `tests['<name>'].result == 'PASS' AND tests['<other>'].result == 'PASS'`. Free-form so workspaces can adopt their own DSL.

result.description: Last-computed verdict. Mirrors the gate_status enum so the dashboard can lift it directly. Null when the evaluator has not been run yet.

blocked_by.description: POPULATED — list of behavior_test names whose failure or absence is blocking the gate. Empty when result is 'passed'.

evaluated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last evaluation. Lets the dashboard mark the verdict as stale when behind a fresher simulation.

simulation_set.description: Section 3 of the canonical 8-section structure. Replaces v3 variants + interventions. Each entry is a concrete run plan. Pass B adds an optional `kind:` discriminator: when absent or `single`, the existing free-form single-run shape applies (back-compat). When `kind: sweep`, the entry is a structured parameter sweep with axes, candidates, and rejection reasons (see the Pass B sweep-table spec). Array shape preferred; object shape accepted for forward compatibility.

oneOf.description: Single-run entry (kind absent or kind: single). Free-form per the v3/v4 single-simulation shape.

kind.description: Optional discriminator. Absent defaults to single.

perturbation.description: null for no perturbation; otherwise an object whose keys describe what to override.

status.description: Common values: ready, gated. Free-form for forward compat.

oneOf.description: Sweep entry (kind: sweep). Structured Cartesian-product parameter sweep. axes + name are required; runs/aggregate_metrics/candidates_selected/rejection_reasons are populated AFTER execution.

base_model.description: Optional; inherits from study if absent.

axes.description: One or more swept axes. Multiple axes = Cartesian product.

duration_min.description: Alternate duration in minutes (matches single-run shape).

metrics.description: Names of metrics captured per run.

pass_fail_tests.description: behavior_tests slugs to apply per run.

candidate_selection.description: Free-form strategy for picking winning runs.

runs.description: POPULATED AFTER EXECUTION. Each entry: {axis_values: {...}, seed: int, run_id: str, metrics: {...}, test_results: {test_name: pass|fail}}.

artifact_path.description: Optional path to CSV/JSON dump on disk.

status.description: Common values: ready, gated, ran. Free-form for forward compat.

model_change.description: Section 4 of the canonical 8-section structure (paired with implementation_requirements). What code changes in the composite. Object shape is preferred; string shape accepted for terse summaries.

key_assumptions.description: Cross-cutting field paired with section 4 (Build). Short biological context — assumption strings. Long-form lit review belongs in references/notes/<bib-key>.md, not here.

readouts.description: Section 5 of the canonical 8-section structure. Replaces v3 observables. Each readout declares what is collected from a simulation run and from where, plus its availability status. Canonical new studies use index_by + optional aggregate; older studies may use identifier or store_path (both tolerated via additionalProperties:true).

store_path.description: DEPRECATED (tolerated for back-compat). Dotted store path to the observable. Use index_by for new readouts. Common values: 'bulk.ID[c]', 'listeners.path.to.field', 'derived'.

identifier.description: DEPRECATED (tolerated for back-compat). Free-text identifier string (dnaa-style). Parsed by readout_resolver into a canonical selector. Common forms: 'listeners.path[int]', 'bulk ID[c]', 'bulk A[c] / (B[c] + C[c])'. Use index_by for new readouts.

index_by.description: Canonical selector: identifier-system lookup. `type` names the catalog; `value` is the key. New readouts should use this form. Extra fields allowed for workspace-specific dialects.

type.description: Identifier catalog name. Canonical enum: bulk_id | monomer_id | listener_id | literal_index. Free-form for workspace-specific catalogs (additionalProperties:true).

value.description: String for symbolic IDs (e.g. 'PD03831[c]'), integer for literal_index lookups.

aggregate.description: Optional aggregation operator applied to the selected series before evaluation. Only set this when the study explicitly requires an aggregation; do NOT infer it from prose annotations. Evaluator (#6) applies this after selecting the observable.

op.description: Aggregation operation. sum | mean | max | min.

over.description: Optional list of observable IDs to aggregate over (for multi-observable sums).

status.description: Availability/classification of this readout. Common values: primary (main observable for this study), measured (supporting observable), available (plumbed today), derived-needed (needs a listener), aspirational (deferred). Kebab-case enforced via pattern; values are free-form for forward compat.

notes.description: Human-readable notes about this readout (biological context, data source, etc.).

cites.description: bib_keys (in references/papers.bib) sourcing the acceptance band or observable value for this readout.

behavior_tests.description: Section 6 of the canonical 8-section structure. Replaces v3 expected_behavior. Note the field is named behavior_tests because `tests:` is dashboard-v4 reserved (for the auto_discover/data_source/pytest_args config block). Each test maps a (measure, pass_if) pair onto a named simulation in simulation_set.

classification.description: Common values: primary, supporting, diagnostic, regression. Free-form for forward compat.

calibration_anchor.description: Optional block surfacing literature-vs-model divergences as part of the test, not just pass/fail. Lets the dashboard show whether a failing test is a calibration problem, a threshold-vs-reality mismatch, or a conceptual disagreement.

observed_value.description: Value measured against the live model (populated by /pbg-study preview-viz or run-tests). Numeric in most cases; string allowed for categorical outcomes.

literature_target.description: Target value drawn from the cited literature (typically the midpoint of the expected band). Hand-authored.

divergence_factor.description: Auto-computed: |observed_value - literature_target| (or observed/literature for ratio scales). The dashboard renders an alarm when this exceeds the workspace's threshold.

resolution.description: Expert verdict on what to do about the divergence. `model` = recalibrate the model; `thresholds` = widen the behavior_test band; `concept` = the test or hypothesis itself needs revision; `unresolved` = pending expert review.

cites.description: Bib keys backing the literature_target. May overlap with the parent test's cites.

conclusion_logic.description: Cross-cutting field paired with section 6 (Tests). Explicit if-pass / if-fail mapping; separates implementation success from biological validation.

limitations.description: Section 7 of the canonical 8-section structure. Explicit out-of-scope claims. Prevents overclaim from a passing test.

implementation_requirements.description: Section 8 of the canonical 8-section structure (paired with model_change). Concrete actionable items. Array shape preferred; object shape accepted for forward compatibility.

kind.description: Common values: listener, parameter_hook, process, data. Free-form for forward compat.

effort.description: Common values: XS, S, M, L, XL.

bibliography.description: Renamed from references because `references:` is dashboard-v4-reserved (list of {file} shape). Keys are section names (e.g. 'expert', 'bib_keys'), values are lists of strings. Free-form additional sections allowed.

followup_proposals.description: Decide-phase output. Each entry is a proposal for a follow-up study motivated by a gap this study revealed. Lifted into a child study via /pbg-study seed-from-followup, which stamps the child with `seeded_from:` and auto-adds the parent to its `pipeline_gate.prerequisites`.

id.description: Slug, unique within this study's followup_proposals.

motivation.description: What gap in this study motivates the follow-up.

hypothesized_mechanism.description: Optional. The missing biology/process the follow-up would add. Becomes the seeded child's model_change: if no explicit seed.model_change is provided.

status.description: Lifecycle of the proposal itself. Flipped to `seeded` automatically by /pbg-study seed-from-followup.

seeded_study.description: Set when status == seeded. Slug of the child study created from this proposal.

seed.description: Optional. Verbatim seed fields transferred to the child study.yaml at seed time. Any shape accepted; common keys mirror the v3 study fields below.

findings.description: First-class study findings (Pass A + Pass 10A; see notes/2026-05-16-findings-protocol.md). Each finding states something we learned and (optionally) ties it back to literature, expert references, and full reproducibility provenance. Required fields (Pass 10A): id, kind, status, statement. All other sub-objects are optional with loose internal shape (`additionalProperties: true`) so authors can experiment with fields beyond the minimum enforced here.

id.description: Stable slug — typically `F-01`, `F-02`, ... — unique within this study.

kind.description: Pass 10A. What category of claim this finding makes. biological = a claim about the modeled system's biology (matches/contradicts/extends literature). computational = a software/wiring/listener bug or correctness finding. methodological = a finding about how studies should be run (sample size, aggregation, evaluation pipeline).

status.description: Pass 10A. How the finding relates to its expected reference. confirms = model matches expectation. partial = model matches some aspects. contradicts = model diverges from expectation. novel = no prior expectation; finding surfaces something new.

statement.description: One-paragraph English claim. The headline a reader scans.

evidence.description: Pass 10A. Optional. Where the finding came from. Common keys: from_run (run name), from_test (behavior_test name), observed (scalar/string measurement), units, window, reduction, smoking_gun (log/trace/diff), discovered_during (free-form provenance note). All sub-fields optional and loose.

expected.description: Pass 10A. Optional. The literature/reference comparator the finding is being judged against. Common keys: cites (list of bib_keys from references/papers.bib), range ([low, high]) OR threshold (number), summary (one-paragraph plain-English reference claim). All sub-fields optional and loose.

expert_reference.description: Pass 10A. Optional. Pointer into a workspace expert PDF. Common keys: doc (expert_doc id from workspace.yaml.expert_docs), section (e.g. '§4.1'), quote (verbatim PDF excerpt), note (curator gloss when no clean quote exists). All sub-fields optional and loose.

explanation.description: Pass 10A. Optional. Multi-paragraph rationale — why we believe the finding, alternatives ruled out.

next_action.description: Pass 10A. Optional. Concrete one-liner — often 'Seed follow-up X' or 'Re-run with Y'.

obsoleted_by.description: Pass 10A. Optional. ID of a later finding that supersedes this one. Lets findings be append-only across runs while still chaining staleness.

provenance.description: Pass A finding_provenance. Full reproducibility metadata for a single finding. All sub-fields optional — populate what you have. Lets a reader (or downstream linter) check whether the finding is still backed by the current artifacts.

run_ids.description: IDs of runs (from runs.db) the finding was extracted from.

simulation_config_hash.description: Hash of the simulation config (composite + params) used to produce the underlying runs.

model_commit_hash.description: Git commit of the model/composite code that produced the underlying runs.

parca_cache_hash.description: Hash of the parca/sim_data cache used (when relevant).

random_seeds.description: Random seeds the underlying runs were executed with.

analysis_script.description: Path or dotted function reference to the analysis code that derived the finding from raw output.

evaluator_version.description: Version string of the evaluator/scorer that produced the finding.

raw_data_artifact.description: Path or URI to raw output (e.g. runs.db path, emitter SQLite, NumPy archive).

metric_table_artifact.description: Path or URI to a structured metric table summarizing the finding (e.g. CSV/Parquet).

seeded_from.description: Provenance stamp set automatically by /pbg-study seed-from-followup when this study is created from a parent's followup_proposals entry. Queryable lineage: `grep -r 'seeded_from' studies/*/study.yaml` finds all seeded children. Pass 10B adds an optional `finding:` field, populated when the child was seeded from a specific finding via `--from-finding <id>`; this lets follow-up lineage trace not just which proposal seeded which child but which *finding* on the parent motivated the follow-up.

study.description: Parent study slug. Optional individually (back-compat; Pass 8 + Pass 10B treat all three sub-fields as independently optional).

proposal_id.description: Proposal id within the parent's followup_proposals list. Optional individually.

finding.description: Pass 10B. Optional. Finding id (e.g. `F-03`) on the parent study that motivated this seeded child. Set automatically when `/pbg-study seed-from-followup --from-finding <id>` is used. Enables queries like `grep -r 'finding: F-' studies/*/study.yaml` to trace finding-to-followup lineage.

follow_up_of.description: v4 narrative field. Slug of the study this one is a parallel/cleanup follow-up of (e.g. dnaa-02f follow_up_of: dnaa-02). Distinct from `seeded_from` (which is set automatically by /pbg-study seed-from-followup) and from `parent_studies` (which is a DAG dependency). Lets a study explicitly mark itself as a sibling that runs in parallel to resolve an unresolved tension surfaced by the parent.

runtime.description: v4 narrative field. Per-study runtime/execution overrides. Read by the dashboard's run handlers. All sub-fields optional — populate only what differs from workspace defaults.

subprocess_timeout_s.description: Override the workspace subprocess timeout for this study's runs. Useful for slow-cycle conditions (e.g. acetate) where the default 600s is too short.

default_emitter.description: Per-study emitter override. Defaults to workspace.yaml.runtime.default_emitter (which itself defaults to sqlite). Set to xarray when the study wires a multi-generation zarr pipeline.

max_generations.description: Optional cap on multi-generation runs. Framework default is single-generation (run_with_division stops at first division). Set this when the workspace owns its own multi-gen loop (e.g. v2ecoli/library/xarray_run.py).

post_run_scripts.description: Dotted Python paths or executable script paths to invoke after each run completes. Each entry receives the runs.db row + the run's emit_paths. Used for in-workspace post-processing without bloating the framework run path.

enforced_params.description: v4 narrative field. Composite-parameter values the study REQUIRES be applied (param_enforcement gate). When set, the framework verifies after each run that the declared parameters match what the composite actually applied; mismatches surface as `ParamViolation` entries on the run row + a banner on the report. Accepts either the flat shape `{composite_param: expected_value, ...}` or the wrapped shape `{params: {...}, source: '<bib-key or PDF citation>'}` for traceability — the helper `load_enforced_params()` in pbg_superpowers/param_enforcement.py detects which shape was used. The schema is intentionally permissive (additionalProperties: true) because both shapes overlap and the framework owns the parsing.

conditions.description: v4 narrative field — the conditions block. Replaces top-level baseline/variants/observables for v4 specs, alongside `model_settings` (tunable parameter catalog with current/default/range/cites) and `expert_inputs` (one-off knobs an expert can twist via the dashboard). The lib/spec_migration.py `_project_v4_redesign_to_legacy_view` projects this to the legacy v3 view for code paths that haven't been updated yet, so v4 specs work everywhere a v3 spec does.

baseline.description: v4 baseline is a single object (not a list). For multiple baseline composites in v4, use a v3 spec or use sweep-style simulation_set entries.

variants.description: Same shape as v3 top-level variants[].

model_settings.description: Tunable parameter catalog. Each entry: {name, default, current, range: [low, high], cites: [bib_keys], notes}. Lets the dashboard show 'this study is configured with X = 0.046 (default 0.046, lit range [0.01, 0.1] per Boesen2024)' for every knob, and lets the param_enforcement linter compare current vs default for drift.

expert_inputs.description: One-off knobs an expert can twist via the dashboard's Study Inputs tab — feature flags, mechanism toggles, calibration choices. Each entry: {name, kind: toggle|enum|number, current, options?, description}.

report.description: v4 narrative field — the executive summary block. Lands at the top of the rendered study page. Lets a reviewer who won't read the YAML or runs.db see the headline verdict, evidence quality, and key numbers in one panel. dnaa-02 example: {title, verdict: failing-bio, confidence: high, evidence_quality: calibrated, objective: '...', conclusion: '...', main_insight: '...', caveat: '...', key_metrics: [{label, value, status}]}.

verdict.description: Free-form headline verdict. Common values: passing | passing-with-caveats | failing-bio | failing-impl | inconclusive | not-yet-run.

evidence_quality.description: Common values: calibrated (verified against literature targets) | literature-matched (qualitative match) | aspirational (target stated, not yet measured) | regression-only (only structural checks).

key_metrics.description: Each entry: {label, value, status} or a free-form string. Rendered as a small table next to the verdict.

study_card.description: v4 narrative field — one-paragraph dashboard card. Distinct from `report.objective` (which is multi-sentence). dnaa-02 example: {goal: 'Split DnaA into apo/ATP/ADP...', mechanism: 'New bulk species + a first-order hydrolysis Step', why_before_next: 'dnaa-03 needs to know which nucleotide form...', expected_result: 'DnaA-ATP / total in [0.2, 0.5]', main_expert_question: 'v2ecoli already has a MONOMER0-160_RXN — should we reuse it?'}.

why_before_next.description: Why this study must finish before the next one in the DAG.

main_expert_question.description: The one question the study most wants an expert to weigh in on. Surfaced in the expert-review panel.

biological_summary.description: v4 narrative field — multi-paragraph plain-English mechanism summary. Distinct from `report.main_insight` (one sentence) and `study_card.mechanism` (one paragraph). This is the 'textbook write-up' a non-modeler would read to understand what the study is about. Markdown allowed.

literature_anchors.description: v4 narrative field — testable expectations from the literature, paired with how they're measured in the model. Each entry pins a literature claim to a model observable and tracks its current status in the workspace. Lets a reviewer audit 'did we actually implement this?' without reading code.

expectation.description: What the literature claims (e.g. 'DnaA-ATP / total DnaA ≈ 20-50% during steady growth').

model_observable.description: How to measure this in the simulation (e.g. 'bulk[DnaA_ATP] / (bulk[DnaA_apo] + bulk[DnaA_ATP] + bulk[DnaA_ADP])').

source.description: Bib-key or short literature citation (e.g. 'Boesen 2024 PNAS', 'Skarstad 1986').

status_in_workspace.description: Free-form; common values: 'Not yet measurable — species don't exist yet' | 'Already available via X listener' | 'Partial — only Y is plumbed' | 'Verified — observed value matches'.

cites.description: Optional bib_key list (preferred over the free-form `source` for new entries).

design_pivot_required.description: v4 narrative field — named open decision points the study has hit, with their candidate paths. Lets an expert see what choices are blocking forward progress instead of guessing from prose. dnaa-02 example: {id: dnaa-02-EQ-04, status: superseded-by-dnaa-02f, question: 'How do we make hydrolysis stick when MONOMER0-4565_RXN re-equilibrates?', alternatives: ['A. Add locked species', 'B. Patch stoichMatrix'], requested_response: 'Expert opinion on (A) vs (B)'}.

id.description: Stable slug, unique within this study (e.g. dnaa-02-EQ-04).

status.description: Common values: open | accepted | rejected | superseded-by-<slug> | obsolete | resolved.

requested_response.description: What the author wants from a reviewer (e.g. 'Expert opinion on whether (A) or (B) is cleaner').

conclusion_verdicts.description: v4 narrative field — three-track verdict block. Distinct from `conclusion_logic` (which is the if-pass/if-fail decision tree). dnaa established three separable dimensions a study can pass/fail on: regression_compatibility (does the code still build/run cleanly?), biological_validation (does it match the literature?), explanatory_gain (did we learn something new?). Each track is {result: PASS|FAIL|MIXED|PENDING, basis: '<why>'}. Lets the dashboard render a 'PASS on regression but MIXED on biology' verdict instead of forcing one boolean.
